what_it_does: Acceptance test for the activation-context registry and three non-turn trigger paths (background, sense, rollup). It exercises per-turn baseline behavior, mode-specific allocation, slot-budget capping, the sacred per-turn reserve floor, and verifies the registry is reflected in runtime/AGENTS.md.
observations: Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts and prints ok lines.
fresh_suite() builds a temporary FsStore, discovers nodes, and returns a Suite instance for each section.
Starts with drift checks: reads runtime/AGENTS.md and asserts ACTIVATION_CONTEXTS, each context, ACTIVATION_ALLOCATION, and reserve-floor language are documented.
Inspects the registry shape: each context must declare trigger, reply, fires_swarm, destinations, and owned_by.
Verifies no activation context destination overlaps with FORBIDDEN_DESTINATION_VERBS (the G9 claude -p floor).
C5.1 treats per-turn as the chat/chat_parts spine that cannot be fired via fire_activation and is live in every mode.
C5.2 fires background in listening mode, asserts no chat events, checks cognition.wave run-records, and reads back landed run://<turn>/<role> content.
C5.3 fires sense with a synthetic event and asserts it fails loud without an event.

C5.4 consolidates prior cognition.wave records into a rollup at run://rollup/, checks distributions, persistence, and that gc=True reports reclaimable refs without shortening the append-only event log.

C5.5 compares listening, walkthrough, and background allocations; derives SlotBudgets from registry; monkey-patches _cog.run_role to prove a 6-role cast is capped at swarm_slots=2; and uses a live semaphore adversarial test to show background saturation cannot starve the per-turn reserve.
standouts: Live concurrent semaphore adversarial proof that a saturating background wave leaves the per-turn reserve immediately acquirable, plus a teeth check that holding all max_num_seqs permits blocks per-turn.
Monkey-patching _cog.run_role with stub roles and a contrived SlotBudget to empirically verify in-flight concurrency capping without calling the resident model.
Rollup treated as a read-half consolidation that emits a cognition.rollup record and reports reclaimable refs, while explicitly not deleting append-only events.
conventions: Follows a numbered acceptance-test structure (C5.1–C5.5) that maps to a design spec.
Uses functional end-to-end assertions against real Suite/store/registry behavior rather than unit mocks, except where concurrency cap proof requires stubbed roles.
Reuses existing subsystems (run_swarm, SlotBudget, rules.route, run_stats) and asserts reuse rather than forked implementations.
Mirrors prior acceptance patterns: drift check against AGENTS.md and a PASS counter with per-check print output.
concerns: Relies on global mutable state (_cog.run_role, _cog.global_vram_gate) and threading, making tests sensitive to execution order and external process state.
Adversarial semaphore test uses private API (gate._sem) and fixed timeouts, which may be flaky under load.
Under-reserve test mutates Suite.ACTIVATION_ALLOCATION through an instance attribute override in a way that may not actually affect class-level behavior as intended.
Several assertions catch bare ValueError and treat any ValueError as proof of the intended failure mode, which could mask unrelated errors.
GC assertion only checks reported reclaimable refs and log length, not actual storage reclamation, because the store is append-only by design.
notes: A heavy acceptance test that tries to prove non-turn cognition triggers, mode budgets, and reserve protection both by registry inspection and by live concurrent behavior.
questions: What is the exact schema of act.ACTIVATION_CONTEXTS and Suite.ACTIVATION_ALLOCATION?
How is _cog.global_vram_gate shared across modes and fire_activation calls in production?
What are the precise semantics of run:// addresses, store.head(), and store.get_content()?
What does runtime.rules.FORBIDDEN_DESTINATION_VERBS contain and how is it enforced?
How does fire_activation route outcomes to surface/address/lane destinations?
What is the resident 4B and how is it loaded/configured for these tests?
What is the intended lifecycle of gc_reclaimable refs and when does the store policy actually reclaim them?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it is an acceptance test for G5 activation contexts, registry, non-turn triggers, and reserve/budget invariants.
apparent_themes: Sacred per-turn reserve and G9 floor by construction
Mode-specific activation allocation and slot budgeting
Concurrency safety through a shared VRAM/semaphore gate
Drift testing: code registry must be reflected in AGENTS.md
Reuse of existing runtime subsystems rather than parallel implementations
intention_signals: The file is trying to provide executable proof that the activation-context system can fire casts without a user turn, stay within mode-specific GPU budgets, protect the per-turn reserve from background saturation, and keep its registry documented in the drift home.
novelty: Combines registry-level acceptance checks with a live threaded adversarial semaphore experiment to demonstrate reserve protection, and treats rollup GC as reporting reclaimable refs on an append-only store without deletion.
contribution: An acceptance-test file that documents and enforces the activation-context registry contract, the three non-turn trigger paths, mode allocation differences, slot-budget capping, per-turn reserve protection, and drift reflection to AGENTS.md.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test exercising the activation-context registry, background/sense/rollup non-turn triggers, per-turn reserve floor, mode-specific slot budgets, and drift reflection in runtime/AGENTS.md.
intention_for_embedding: To prove by use that non-turn cognition triggers fire casts safely under mode budgets while preserving the sacred per-turn reserve and keeping the registry documented.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an ok line.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts cond is truthy, increments the module-level PASS counter, and prints a success message; on failure it raises with the label.

symbols.does: Factory that creates an isolated Suite with a temporary FsStore and discovered nodes.
symbols.description: fresh_suite() builds a new temporary directory, initializes an FsStore, discovers nodes from the nodes directory, and returns a fresh Suite instance for each test section.

symbols.does: Minimal role stub used only for the concurrency-cap adversarial test.
symbols.description: class _StubRole provides only id, spec, can_fire=True, and is_jury=False so run_swarm can schedule it without invoking the real model or resident 4B.

symbols.does: Initializes a stub role with a generated id and empty spec.
symbols.description: __init__(self, i) sets self.id to f'stub{i}' and self.spec to an empty dict, completing the minimal role interface needed by run_swarm.

symbols.does: Thread-safe fake role runner that records peak in-flight concurrency.
symbols.description: _fake_run_role(role, ctx, **kw) increments a protected in-flight counter, sleeps briefly to hold the slot, decrements the counter, and returns a dummy result so the cap test can observe peak concurrency.

symbols.does: Thread worker that holds one swarm slot until released.
symbols.description: _bg_holder() acquires a permit from the global VRAM gate, signals via a barrier that the slot is held, and waits on an event so the test can saturate the swarm sub-pool and then probe the reserve.

symbols.does: Thread worker that holds one of all max_num_seqs permits until released.
symbols.description: _full_holder() acquires a permit from the global VRAM gate, signals via a barrier, and waits so the test can demonstrate that holding the entire pool blocks the per-turn acquire.
